response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with strong software craftsmanship, testing culture, and some high-throughput systems exposure, but he falls well short of the core minimum qualifications for this role. His C++ experience amounts to roughly a year (National Instruments plus thesis work) versus the required 8 years, and his large-scale infrastructure experience (~2 years at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, in DLang) is below the 5-year bar. His career centers on full-stack web development rather than cloud compute platforms, and he shows mentoring activity but no formal multi-team technical leadership of the kind this position demands.
strengths: Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus teaching/workshop experience showing mentoring aptitude
Broad technical range and ownership, including founding and operating a Kubernetes-deployed open-source platform
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ minimum — only ~7 months of professional C/C++ work
Limited distributed-systems/infrastructure depth (~2 years) versus the 5-year requirement, and no cloud compute platform experience
No formal technical-lead role; leadership evidence is limited to workshops, katas, and a side-project founder role

key_factors.factor: Strong engineering craft and mentoring aptitude
